name: Slovak Loska (Langoshe) (Pagach?)
recipeInstructions:
1. Blend the flour into the mashed potatoes. Add more flour if needed, but not too much. The dough should be firm and not sticky or crumbly.
2. Divide the dough into four portions. Roll each portion out on a floured board to about 1/8 inch thickness in 8-inch circles.
3. Prick the surface of each circle with a fork to prevent puffing during baking.
4. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Bake the loska on the bottom rack of the oven, using cookie sheets if available, for about 7 minutes on each side.
6. After baking, brush each side with either plain or browned butter.
7. Stack the loska on a plate and cut into wedges. This dish pairs well with any meatless soup.
8. As a child, my grandmother used to call this Pagach.
